display.purpose: The masthead 'Light it Up' only — Archivo Black, signage scale.

title.purpose: Big stat values; Hanken Grotesk 700 with tabular figures.

body.purpose: Subhead and prose; Hanken Grotesk 400, measure ~52ch.

label.purpose: Uppercase tracked micro-text: stat labels, tag, search label.

shadows.name: bulb-glow-1
shadows.purpose: Emitted light on a low-value played cell.

shadows.name: bulb-glow-4
shadows.purpose: Emitted light on the brightest (7+ base) cell.

shadows.name: bulb-glow-hover
shadows.purpose: Hover bloom on a played cell.

shadows.name: focus-halo
shadows.purpose: Soft phosphor halo on the focused search field.

motion.name: reveal-step
motion.purpose: Per-cell stagger for the 1→N ignite sweep.

motion.name: reveal-cap
motion.purpose: Hard ceiling on the total ignite sweep, even for 162 slots.

components.name: Heatmap Cell
components.description: The signature bulb. One slot per team game: hollow socket = not yet played, slate = did not play, ignited by luminance when played.

components.name: Search Field
components.description: Slate field with a leading glyph that turns phosphor on focus, plus a soft halo.

components.name: Suggestion Row
components.description: A flush listbox row; active/hover is a phosphor wash with the team abbrev turning phosphor.

components.name: Nameplate Stat
components.description: A scoreboard stat: phosphor tabular value over an uppercase faint label.

components.name: Tag
components.description: A phosphor-wash metadata pill (never a button).

components.name: Tooltip Chip
components.description: The broadcast data chip shown on a played cell: matchup in phosphor, batting line in dim ink.

components.name: Alert
components.description: A warning-track error panel with a leading glowing dot. Never flashes.
components.html: No 2026 games for that player yet — check back after Opening Day.

narrative.overview: This is an incandescent stadium scoreboard after dark. The page is the black field at night; a player's season ignites across it as a panel of bulbs, each one's brightness scaled to the total bases of a single game. The whole system exists to make one thing — the heatmap — feel alive and worth looking at, the way a scoreboard does when the home team is hot. Energy comes from a single committed signal color and the way light reads off a dark field, not from ornament. Everything that isn't the board stays quiet so the board can shout. The system explicitly rejects the generic SaaS dashboard (gradient-card grids, the hero-metric template, cookie-cutter admin chrome) and the cluttered stat sheet (ESPN / Baseball-Reference table overload). It is also a deliberate move away from its own origin: a GitHub-contributions grid in default greens on white. Here the grid is reframed as a stadium board — warm, emissive, and dark.
keyCharacteristics: One hero (the heatmap), one signal color (phosphor green), one dark field.
Intensity is carried by luminance, never hue alone — colorblind-safe by construction.
Athletic, signage-grade display type over disciplined, legible body type.
Light, not shadow: depth is tonal layering plus the glow a bulb emits.

rules.name: The Luminance Rule
rules.body: The Ignition Ramp is read by brightness, not hue. Every step is lighter than the last. Never encode the value of a game in hue alone — a red-green colorblind viewer must still read the board correctly.

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Phosphor green is the only saturated color on the surface. It means 'live / focused / this is the data.' If you find yourself reaching for phosphor as decoration, stop — use a slate or an ink tone instead.

rules.name: The Three-State Rule
rules.body: Every slot is exactly one of: played (lit bulb on the Ignition ramp — tooltip + keyboard focus, including Ignition 0 for a hitless game), did not play (#283440 filled slate — hover tooltip, not focusable), or not yet played (#0e151b hollow socket — inert). The board lays out one slot per team game in schedule order, so reading the season as played / missed / upcoming is the point.

rules.name: The Scoreboard Weight Rule
rules.body: Archivo Black appears exactly twice — the masthead and the nameplate. Body, labels, and stats are always regular Archivo.

rules.name: The Tabular Rule
rules.body: Every number uses tabular figures. Scoreboards don't let digits wobble.

rules.name: The Light-Not-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat and separated by tone. When you need depth, ask whether the element emits (a bulb → glow) or floats (a menu → drop shadow). Decorative ambient shadows on flat panels are forbidden.

dos: Do let the heatmap be the hero — give it the most room and quiet everything else.
Do carry game value with luminance, and pair color with a non-color signal (the tooltip's exact number).
Do keep phosphor green rare and meaningful (live data, focus, links).
Do reserve Archivo Black for the masthead and nameplate only.
Do convey depth with tone and emitted glow, not ambient drop shadows.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion: the ignite sweep collapses to an instant swap.
Do keep AA contrast and keep every interactive slot keyboard-reachable with a visible focus ring.
donts: Don't build a generic SaaS dashboard: no gradient-card grids, no hero-metric template, no admin-panel chrome.
Don't build a cluttered stat sheet: no ESPN / Baseball-Reference table overload.
Don't regress to the Tailwind-placeholder look (gray-on-white, default green focus ring, system fonts).
Don't use gradient text — solid ink only; emphasis via weight and size.
Don't encode a game's value in hue alone, and never give a future-game socket a tooltip, glow, or keyboard focus.
Don't add decorative glassmorphism or ambient shadows on flat panels.
